northStar: Warm charcoal workbench with code syntax highlights. The core experience is dark and functional, highlighted by colorful code snippets.

dos: Prioritize GT America for all textual elements except for specific code blocks, using ui-monospace for technical content.
Use 0px border-radius for all interactive buttons to maintain a precise, angular aesthetic.
Apply 'Workbench Gradient' (#221f1d to rgba(41, 57, 105, 0.9)) for rich, dark, interactive code editor backgrounds.
Maintain a primary text color of Carbon (#141414) on light backgrounds and Cloud (#ffffff) on dark backgrounds for readability.
Employ the code syntax colors (Code Violet, Pink, Green, Yellow) exclusively for code snippets or specific data highlights to prevent visual noise.
Utilize Ash (#f6f6f6) for light page backgrounds and Slate (#292929) for dark card/surface backgrounds, clearly defining section depth.
Enforce a hierarchical typography scale, with the largest display text at 56px and -2.8px letter spacing, creating a distinct visual voice.
donts: Avoid using rounded corners for buttons; all buttons must have 0px border-radius.
Do not introduce new chromatic colors outside the defined accent palette for syntax highlighting; maintain the established code color scheme.
Do not use generic system fonts for headlines or body text; GT America is fundamental to the brand identity.
Avoid excessive use of shadows; prefer distinct background colors and gradients to indicate elevation or separate surfaces.
Do not dilute the contrast between text and background; ensure high contrast ratios like #ffffff on #141414 or #f6f6f6 on #000000.
Do not mix 'Parchment' (#fdefd2) with 'Ash' (#f6f6f6) on adjacent surfaces; these subtle neutrals serve distinct light-theme background roles.

colors.name: Carbon
colors.role: Primary background for dark mode sections, heading text on light backgrounds, primary text in many contexts.

colors.name: Cloud
colors.role: Primary text on dark backgrounds, icon fills, key interactive elements in light sections.

colors.name: Ash
colors.role: Light mode page backgrounds, input fields, header background.

colors.name: Parchment
colors.role: Subtle light background in main content areas of some light mode sections, providing a warm off-white.

colors.name: Slate
colors.role: Card backgrounds, secondary dark surfaces, dark mode interactive element backgrounds.

colors.name: Stone
colors.role: Divider lines, subtle borders, inactive element text.

colors.name: Whisper
colors.role: Subtle borders, text in low contrast areas, outlines.

colors.name: Code Violet
colors.role: Syntax highlighting, occasional decorative text, drawing attention in code blocks.

colors.name: Code Pink
colors.role: Syntax highlighting for declarations and keywords, drawing attention in code blocks.

colors.name: Code Green
colors.role: Syntax highlighting for string literals, creating visual parsing of code.

colors.name: Code Yellow
colors.role: Syntax highlighting for specific variable types or functions, providing tertiary color coding.

colors.name: Warm Code Blue
colors.role: Action button background on dark mode, indicating primary interaction.

colors.name: Burnt Orange
colors.role: Highlighting interactive elements within code editors, adding warmth and focus.

colors.name: Deep Plum
colors.role: Subtle background for active tabs or secondary interactive states in a coding context.

colors.name: Workbench Gradient
colors.role: Background for code editors and terminal-like areas, creating a dimensional, immersive coding environment.

designSystem.layout: The site uses a mixed layout approach. The initial hero section is dark and full-bleed with a centered headline and action buttons. Subsequent sections alternate between light and dark themes, often using a max-width contained grid for content, centered to a pageMaxWidth of approximately 1200px. Content arrangement frequently features split layouts (text on one side, visual on the other) or stacked content blocks. Vertical spacing between sections is generous and consistent (e.g., 940-1392px gaps). The navigation is a sticky top bar with logo, links, and action buttons. Feature sections often employ a 2-column layout or a card grid structure (e.g., pricing tiers). The density is varied, with spacious hero sections and more compact, information-dense blocks for features and explanations.
designSystem.imagery: The visual language blends illustrative elements with minimalist product UI. Product screenshots are typically dark-themed code editor environments, often presented within a rounded-corner frame, signifying a working digital tool. Illustrations are abstract, pixelated, and geometric, featuring brand colors against light or dark backgrounds (e.g., the grid pattern with scattered orange and black squares), adding a playful yet technical feel. These are often used as decorative atmospheric elements, not functional content. Icons are minimal, either filled white on dark or filled dark on light, without apparent outlines, primarily serving UI navigation and feature indication. The overall density is balanced, with imagery serving as visual breaks or context for text-dominant sections, without overwhelming the content.

similar.why: Both brands combine a dark, developer-centric UI with distinctive typography and vibrant code syntax highlighting.
Shares the dark primary theme with code-focused components, including similar syntax highlighting and navigation paradigms.
Exhibits a similar preference for sharp, angular UI elements and a high-contrast, functionally-driven aesthetic.
Features a strong emphasis on code blocks, monospace fonts, and a crisp, modern sans-serif for UI elements.

designSystem.northStar: Warm charcoal workbench with code syntax highlights. The core experience is dark and functional, highlighted by colorful code snippets.

The backend platform
that keeps your app in sync

Start building on Convex today. No credit card required.

Start buildingLearn more
›npm create convex
components.name: Button Group — Primary + Ghost

components.html:
Everything is code

From database schemas to queries, from auth to APIs, express every part of your backend in pure TypeScript. Your backend code lives next to your app code, is typechecked and autocompleted, and is generated by AI with exceptional accuracy.

01

Always in sync

Convex's reactive query engine automatically re-runs queries when underlying data changes, keeping every client perfectly in sync without any polling or manual cache invalidation.

02

Backend built-ins

File storage, full-text search, scheduled jobs, and real-time updates are all built in — no third-party services needed. Ship full-featured apps faster than ever before.

03

components.name: Product Section Banner — Not just a database
Primary Action Button - Dark BG
components.role: Primary call to action on dark backgrounds.
components.description: Background #69bee2 with #141414 text. Square corners (0px radius). Padding 12px horizontal, 12px vertical. Uses GT America, bold (700) at 16px.

components.name: Outline Ghost Button - Dark BG
components.role: Secondary action or navigation on dark themed sections.
components.description: Transparent background with #E5E5E5 text and 1px #E5E5E5 border. Square corners (0px radius). Padding 16px vertical, 6px horizontal. Uses GT America, regular (400) at 16px.

components.name: Outline Ghost Button - Light BG
components.role: Secondary action or navigation on light themed sections.
components.description: Transparent background with #141414 text and 1px #141414 border. Square corners (0px radius). Padding 8px vertical, 6px horizontal. Uses GT America, regular (400).

components.name: Code Window Container
components.role: Displays interactive code examples and snippets.
components.description: Background is 'Workbench Gradient' (#221f1d to rgba(41, 57, 105, 0.9)). Rounded corners (12px radius). Uses ui-monospace at 13px with syntax highlighting colors.

components.name: Dashboard Card
components.role: Displays data or a list of items within the interactive dashboard.
components.description: Background is #1E1C1A. Rounded corners (12px radius). Text is #F6F6F6. Internal padding varies (e.g., 16px). Employs code syntax colors for data entry distinction.

components.name: Search Input Field
components.description: Background #F6F6F6, text #F6F6F6. 0px border radius, with a 1px #F6F6F6 border. Padding 16px all around. Uses GT America.

components.name: Navigation Link - Active
components.role: Indicates current page or active menu item.
components.description: Text color #ffffff on dark navigation bars. Underlined if on dark background with #ffffff color. Font GT America, weight 400.

components.name: Navigation Link - Inactive
components.description: Text color #A9A9AC or #6D6D70. Font GT America, weight 400.

typography.role: The primary brand typeface for all headlines, body text, and UI elements. Its strong geometric sans-serif character defines the modern, precise tone.
Default monospace font for code blocks, terminal output, and technical data displays. Ensures consistent and readable code presentation.

designSystem.description: This design system feels like a developer's workbench with a warm, analog-meets-digital aesthetic. It pairs a dark, almost charcoal-colored UI with hints of muted earth tones and vibrant, isolated code syntax colors. The sharp, unrounded buttons stand in contrast to the subtly rounded general UI elements, creating a precise, tool-like feel, while the extensive use of `GT America` font brings a distinctive modern tech presence.

customSections.title: Agent Prompt Guide
customSections.content:
### Quick Color Reference
- Text (Dark BG): #ffffff (Cloud)
- Text (Light BG): #141414 (Carbon)
- Background (Dark Section): #141414 (Carbon)
- Background (Light Section): #f6f6f6 (Ash)
- CTA (Primary): #69bee2 (Warm Code Blue)
- Accent (Code Pink): #fc618d

### Example Component Prompts
1. Create a dark hero section: `background: #141414 (Carbon)`. Headline: 'The backend platform that keeps your app in sync' `GT America, 56px, weight 700, #ffffff (Cloud), letter-spacing: -2.8px`. Primary CTA button: 'Start building' `background: #69bee2 (Warm Code Blue), text: #141414 (Carbon), radius: 0px, padding: 12px 12px`.
2. Design a `Code Window Container`: `background:` linear-gradient(45deg, rgb(34, 31, 29), rgb(49, 43, 43) 28%, rgba(41, 57, 105, 0.9) 50%) (Workbench Gradient). `border-radius: 12px`. Inner code text: `ui-monospace, 13px, #ffffff (Cloud) with syntax highlights for keywords (#948ae3 - Code Violet), strings (#7bd88f - Code Green), and functions (#fc618d - Code Pink).`
3. Generate an 'Outline Ghost Button (Dark BG)': `background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0), color: #E5E5E5 (Whisper), border: 1px solid #E5E5E5 (Whisper), radius: 0px, padding: 16px 6px`. Text 'npm create convex' `GT America, 16px, weight 400`.
4. Produce a light-themed section card: `background: #fdefd2 (Parchment), border-radius: 12px`. Headline: 'LLMs love Convex' `GT America, 40px, weight 700, #141414 (Carbon), letter-spacing: -1.0px`. Body text: 'With Convex, everything is just TypeScript.' `GT America, 16px, weight 400, #141414 (Carbon)`. Secondary CTA: 'Learn more' `background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0), color: #141414 (Carbon), border: 1px solid #141414 (Carbon), radius: 0px, padding: 8px 6px`.
